deep_dive.title: AC$^2$P$^2$SL: Adaptive Communication-Computation Pipeline Parallel Split Learning over Edge Networks
deep_dive.abstract: In wireless edge networks, split learning (SL) enables base station (BS) to utilize the distributed data and computing power across user equipments (UEs) to achieve collaborative model training while protecting local data privacy. However, the inherent sequential execution of computation and communication processes in conventional SL usually leads to long training times. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an adaptive communication-computation pipeline parallel split learning (AC$^2$P$^2$SL) framework. By conceptualizing the communication and computation processes of UEs and the BS as a unified pipeline, AC$^2$P$^2$SL achieves fine-grained pipeline parallelism across multiple micro-batches. Through this approach, effective overlapping of communication and computation is achieved which results in significant reduction of the overall training latency. Moreover, by considering the system constraints in the communication, computation, and storage dimensions as well as the heterogeneity of UEs, we formulate a joint optimization problem to minimize the training time and propose a corresponding split and pre-allocation algorithm to further enhance the pipeline efficiency. Additionally, accounting for the practical dynamic environments for the UEs, we design an adaptive re-allocation strategy to enhance the system resilience. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of AC$^2$P$^2$SL in reducing training time while ensuring data privacy preservation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: adaptive communication-computation pipeline for efficient split learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自适应通信计算流水线并行可有效减少训练时间
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自适应通信计算流水线并行可有效减少训练时间
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自适应通信计算流水线并行可有效减少训练时间
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AC2P2SL, an adaptive pipeline-parallel split learning framework that overlaps communication and computation across micro-batches to reduce training latency in edge networks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统分裂学习串行执行导致训练时间长的问题，本文提出AC2P2SL框架，通过将UE和基站的通信与计算过程统一为流水线，实现微批次级别的细粒度并行。该框架自适应调整流水线策略，显著缩短训练时长，同时保持隐私保护优势，为无线边缘网络中的高效分布式训练提供了新方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对传统分裂学习串行执行导致训练时间长的问题，本文提出AC2P2SL框架，通过将UE和基站的通信与计算过程统一为流水线，实现微批次级别的细粒度并行。该框架自适应调整流水线策略，显著缩短训练时长，同时保持隐私保护优势，为无线边缘网络中的高效分布式训练提供了新方案。

quick_skim.title: Federated Learning with Energy-Based Structured Probabilistic Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Federated learning typically aggregates client updates using fixed or heuristic weighting rules, which can be suboptimal when clients have heterogeneous data and varying contributions to the global model. We propose a framework that refines client aggregation weights using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). Our method defines unary potentials for individual clients and pairwise potentials for all client pairs, allowing the server to model both client-specific reliability and interactions between clients. The resulting CRF inference produces aggregation weights that enable better convergence of the global training objective. Experiments show that, under non-IID heterogeneity, our approach consistently improves performance over well-established federated learning baselines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: federated learning with structured aggregation weights, improving communication efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦学习中结构化聚合权重优化，提升通信效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 联邦学习中结构化聚合权重优化，提升通信效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 联邦学习中结构化聚合权重优化，提升通信效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a CRF-based aggregation weight refinement for federated learning, improving convergence under non-IID data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对联邦学习中固定或启发式聚合权重在数据异构时的次优问题，本文提出基于条件随机场的聚合权重优化框架。该方法通过一元势和成对势建模客户端可靠性与交互，推理出更优的权重，从而提高全局模型收敛速度。实验表明，在非独立同分布数据下性能优于现有方法。该技术可迁移至分布式图神经网络训练中的客户端聚合。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对联邦学习中固定或启发式聚合权重在数据异构时的次优问题，本文提出基于条件随机场的聚合权重优化框架。该方法通过一元势和成对势建模客户端可靠性与交互，推理出更优的权重，从而提高全局模型收敛速度。实验表明，在非独立同分布数据下性能优于现有方法。该技术可迁移至分布式图神经网络训练中的客户端聚合。
